title: Nerv HQ
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Nerv HQ             Quote Post by TheDrmcvey » Thu Apr 04, 2013 7:01 pm The more I think about the ruins of Nerv, the more I start to speculate about what had actually happened. Yes we know that the last angel in 2.0 caused a lot of damage to HQ, but what caught my eye when watching 3.0 was the bullet holes in some of the walls at Nerv. This leads me to think that maybe after Misato left Nerv, she decided to take on a full-scale assault against Nerv by convincing the Japanese government to use its military to infiltrate Nerv and destroy everything inside. After all, she was a victim of the 2nd Impact, and does not ever want to see another impact happen again. Nerv is responsible for the 4th Impact as seen at the end of 3.0, and maybe even responsible for the 3rd. As she told Shinji before he left, "We are Wille, an organization dedicated to Nerv's annihilation", this convinces me that she had some part in the destruction of Nerv. What do you all think?

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Thu Apr 04, 2013 11:43 pm I don't see why they don't just break into Nerv and destroy everything. They have Mari and Asuka against Rei and Kaworu, and they also have the Wunder. So I think it's safe to say they at least out number them. Even then, they could still do some hefty damage. What I'm really surprised though about Nerv HQ is Gendo and his huge ass office. He must really like it cause in 3.0 there's a giant gaping hole in it, and he's still sitting there like there isn't even a giant hole in the wall. That must be one Swag as Hell office.

[]:             Quote Post by Jadley » Thu Apr 04, 2013 11:53 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: I don't see why they don't just break into Nerv and destroy everything. They have Mari and Asuka against Rei and Kaworu, and they also have the Wunder. So I think it's safe to say they at least out number them. Even then, they could still do some hefty damage. What I'm really surprised though about Nerv HQ is Gendo and his huge ass office. He must really like it cause in 3.0 there's a giant gaping hole in it, and he's still sitting there like there isn't even a giant hole in the wall. That must be one Swag as Hell office.

[]:             Quote Post by TheDrmcvey » Fri Apr 05, 2013 12:15 am Jadley wrote: When you're that cool, you don't bother with details. And they did actually try to break in Nerv's HQ or what's left of it almost at the end of the movie, though their intentions weren't clear, we don't know if they were coming for Shinji or if they were just striking in to destroy everything.

[]:             Quote Post by palerider1911 » Fri Apr 05, 2013 2:22 am I'm one of the people that holds the belief that the previews we saw of Q at the end of 2.0 are things that actually happened during the time-skip. So, with that in mind, the mess we see of Nerv might be the outcome of [the JSDF?] coming and arresting all of the Nerv personnel....as well as for all of the subsequent events during the 14 years...that we'll probably never get to know about. Who knows, it might have even looked something like what we saw during EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Apr 05, 2013 4:00 am There is ONE thing I would really want to know : how Gendo managed to install factories of mass producing Evangelion and Nemesis Series in the middle of NERV HQ without anyone noticing it?! I mean I doubt he just built it with his bare hands with spare parts from the red wasteland after NERV scission, all of this must have to been there before the HQ was brough to ruins!

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Fri Apr 05, 2013 6:19 am ElMariachi wrote: There is ONE thing I would really want to know : how Gendo managed to created factories of mass producing Evangelion and Nemesis Series in the middle of NERV HQ without anyone realizing it?!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Apr 05, 2013 7:04 am Giji Shinka wrote: Simple: Solid Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Fri Apr 05, 2013 7:28 am ElMariachi wrote: What?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Apr 05, 2013 8:22 am Giji Shinka wrote: Metal gear solid reference. You know, the game where solid snake sneaks around without being detected.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Fri Apr 05, 2013 9:01 am I would have to say Gendo and Old Man Fuyu must have had to do it bare handed because no one in WILLE knew about Eva 13 and it's creation pod, and we never see it before, so I assume it had to have been made after 2.0. But if they had the Mark 09 that would have helped the construction, but we don't see the Mark 09 previously either.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Apr 05, 2013 9:12 am Maybe Mark.09 and EVA-13 were created in that secret Moon Base, like Mark.06. After all if there is a secluded place that wasn't been affected by N3I, it's here!

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Fri Apr 05, 2013 10:13 am ElMariachi wrote: Maybe Mark.09 and EVA-13 were created in that secret Moon Base, like Mark.06. After all if there is a secluded place that wasn't been affected by N3I, it's here!

[]:             Quote Post by palerider1911 » Fri Apr 05, 2013 7:41 pm Giji Shinka wrote: If they were built there, They (Adams) had to be there from the start. Cause isn't it rather hard to move 2 Adams to the moon after the end of the world has happened? (Due to the lack of people to work with them ect+ they are HUGE)

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Wed Apr 10, 2013 10:02 pm What about all the other Nerv bases? We know they have the capabilities to build evas, and their personnel wasn't in the know of what happened like Misato & Co., for all they care they would have seen a bunch of red lights and a warning screen the same way Nerv HQ saw it on the "Magi news" that the base in USA vanished. Gendo/Seele could have made up a story to cover up near-3rd Impact (it's not like it's the first time they do just that!) and use the remaining Nerv bases around the world to further their goals, even have some upgrades made to the ruins of Nerv HQ, and of course manufacture Gendo's Keel visor!

[]:             Quote Post by sephiros » Wed Apr 10, 2013 11:13 pm Metal gear solid reference. You know, the game where solid snake sneaks around without being detected.

[]:             Quote Post by Jadley » Thu Apr 11, 2013 12:51 am sephiros wrote: Gendo's in his Outer Heaven, all's right with the world.
